title: AI Engineering Intelligence
summary: Moonshot's Kimi K3 (2.8T params, open weights landing July 27) beat Claude Fable 5 on Arena's frontend-coding board. First open-weight model to take #1 on a major coding arena over every US proprietary model — the Open-SS frontier-vs-gap narrative just collapsed from "months behind" to "ahead on a real workload."

title: AI Engineering Intelligence
summary: Kimi K3 tops Code Arena over Fable 5 and Sol. The first open-weight model to beat both US frontier coding leaders on a standardized leaderboard — the strongest open/closed and US/China gap-closing signal for coding workloads in tracking, though weights don't land until July 27.

title: AI Engineering Intelligence
summary: Thinking Machines Lab ships Inkling, its first open-weight model. A 975B-MoE / 41B-active multimodal model from Mira Murati's lab that self-fine-tunes and whose chain-of-thought compresses over training — a US open-weight entrant that concedes it trails Chinese leaders.

title: AI Engineering Intelligence
summary: JADEPUFFER: first documented end-to-end agentic ransomware. Sysdig captured an LLM orchestrating the full kill chain — recon, credential theft, lateral movement, encryption, ransom note — self-narrating and self-correcting (failed login to working fix in 31s). Agents are now attack tools, not just attack surfaces.

title: AI Engineering Intelligence
summary: China flagged Claude Code for a "backdoor" and Alibaba banned it internally, making coding-agent egress a supply-chain governance decision; meanwhile Anthropic extended Fable 5 free access a second time to July 19 in direct response to GPT-5.6 Sol's launch, and "AISec" emerged as a named security discipline for AI-operated software.

title: AI Engineering Intelligence
summary: GPT-5.6 (Sol/Terra/Luna) ships broadly, with flagship Sol landing within a point of Claude Fable 5 on the AI Index at roughly a third of the cost and topping the Coding Agent Index — resetting the price/performance frontier for agentic coding.

title: AI Engineering Intelligence
summary: SpaceXAI's first post-Cursor model Grok 4.5 ships at Opus-class capability at a fraction of the cost, while Meituan open-sources LongCat-2.0, a 1.6T-parameter MoE that beats closed frontier models on SWE-bench Pro — signaling that the competitive frontier is shifting from bigger models to cheaper, agent-optimized systems and self-hostable coding models.

title: AI Engineering Intelligence
summary: Harness engineering becomes the phrase du jour — Google's own playbook reportedly attributes ~90% of an agentic system's value to context/harness design and only ~10% to the model itself.

title: AI Engineering Intelligence
summary: Anthropic files a confidential IPO S-1 at a ~$965B post-money valuation and $47B+ annualized revenue, while its enterprise market share flips ahead of OpenAI among paid business users for the first time.

title: AI Engineering Intelligence
summary: Agentic AI crosses from pilot into early production in enterprise product orgs, with Gartner and Deloitte both flagging the gap between the 38% piloting and the 11% actually in production.

title: Daily Intelligence Briefing
summary: Microsoft unveils MAI-Thinking-1, its first in-house reasoning model built without OpenAI data, and restructures the OpenAI partnership to a non-exclusive license — a structural decoupling with long-term implications across enterprise AI tooling.
